content.full_text:
May 28, 1975


Mrs. Wayne Scott, President
California Federation of
Republican Woman
16945 Avenida de Santa Ynaz
Pacific Palisades, CA 90272

Dear Mrs. Scott:

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 75-118

Thank you for your letter of May 13, 1975 requesting
an opinion with respect to the duties of the California
Federation of Republican Women under the Political Reform
Act.

Since your request raises no substantial question
involving interpretation of the Political Reform Act,
your request for a formal opinion is denied.

Mass mailings are subject to certain regulations
under Government Code Section 84305. No person may take
an "expenditure" for the purpose of sending a mass
mailing unless the postage is paid by postage meter or
the mail is sent by first class or third class mail. Payment for a mass mailing is an "expenditure"
unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances
that the expenditure is not made for political purposes.
See Government Code Section 82025.

The monthly bulletin of the California Federation
of Republican Women is without question a political publication. The surrounding circumstances make clear that
payments made for its mailing are made for a political
purpose, whether or not a particular issue supports or
opposes candidates. Therefore, the mailing must be sent
by postage meter or bulk rate permit.

Section 84305 also requires a copy of every mass
mailing in support of or in opposition to a state

Mrs. Wayne Scott
May 23, 1975
Page Two


candidate or state measure to be sent to the Commission.
Any issue of the bulletin which is in support of or
opposition to specific state candidates or measures
must be sent to the Commission.

Although for the reason stated no formal opinion
will be issued, I hope the foregoing is sufficiently
helpful. If you have any further questions, I urge
you to contact a member of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,


Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman, for the
Commission

DHL:cjb

Request No. 75 11'
Data Received 5-16-75
Response Due 5-30-75

CALIFORNIA FEDERATION OF REPUBLICAN WOMEN
SOUTHERN DIVISION

PRESIDENT
Mrs. Wayne W. Scott
[illegible address]
[illegible]
[illegible]

FIRST VICE PRESIDENT
Mrs. P. P. Feddersen, Jr.
1651 Cabrillo St.
West Cov.-i, Calif. 91791
(213) 331-3254

SECOND VICE PRESIDENT
Mrs. Edward A. Heiss
12960 Casero Court
Saratoga, Calif. 95070
(714) 447-0533

THIRD VICE PRESIDENT
Mrs. Sidney Donor
2870 M-y Lane
Escondido, Calif. 91316
(714) 745-1723

RECORDING SECRETARY
Mrs. William Shaue
623 Olive St.
Santa Barbara, Calif. 93109
(805) 929-3213

TREASURER
Mrs. Kennuth Warner
153 Bay View Court
Vallejo, Calif. 94360
(805) 435-4846

MEMBERSHIP SECRETARY
Mrs. George Stuart
830 Sycamore Court
Fullerton, Calif. 92635
(714) 529-1892

CORRESPONDING SECRETARY
Mrs. Helen Matlox
22447 Navajo Circle
Torrance, Calif. 90505
(213) 534-1915

PARLIAMENTARIAN
Mrs. Joseph L. Wood
1515 Li Ca-ada Blvd.
La Canada, Calif. 91011
(213) 790-1420

16945 Avenida de Santa Ynez
Pacific Palisades, Ca. 90272

May 13, 1975

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.C. Box 805
Sacramento, Ca. 95804

Dear Sirs:

It has been called to my attention that according to Government Code, § 82041.5, regarding mass mailings to 200 or more, the provision may apply to our organization's monthly bulletin mailed to our officers, board of directors, and club presidents. We do, on a few pages, make recommendations on pending legislation.

For this reason we are enclosing copies of our bulletins for this year and would like your opinions as to whether we are complying, in all respects, to the law and, in the future, if we may just send those pages specifically pertaining to legislation and/or candidates rather than mail the entire bulletin. Our regular mailing goes out under postal permit but is mailed as third class mail.

Your prompt reply will be greatly appreciated.

Sincerely yours,

[signature]
Mrs. Wayne Scott
President

enc.

embedding.qa_text: May 28, 1975 Mrs. Wayne Scott, President California Federation of Republican Women 16945 Avenida de Santa Ynez Pacific Palisades, CA 90272 Dear Mrs. Scott: Re: Request for Opinion No. 75-113 Thank you for your letter of May 13, 1975 requesting an opinion with respect to the duties of the California Federation of Republican Women under the Political Reform Act. Since your request raises no substantial question involving interpretation of the Political Reform Act, your request for a formal opinion is denied. Mass mailings are subject to certain regulations under Government Code Section 84305. No person may make an "expenditure" for the purpose of sending a mass mailing unless the postage is paid by postage meter or the mail is sent by first class or third class bulk rate mail. Payment for a mass mailing is an "expenditure" unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances that the expenditure is not made for political purposes. See Government Code Section 82025. The monthly bulletin of the California Federation of Republican Women is without question a political publication. The surrounding circumstances make clear that payments made for its mailing are made for a political purpose, whether or not a particular issue supports or opposes candidates. Therefore, the mailing must be sent by postage meter or bulk rate permit. Section 84305 also requires a copy of every mass mailing in support of or in opposition to a state Mrs. Wayne Scott May 23, 1975 Page Two candidate or state measure to be sent to the Commission. Any issue of the bulletin which is in support of or opposition to specific state candidates or measures must be sent to the Commission. Although for the reason stated no formal opinion will be issued, I hope the foregoing is sufficiently helpful. If you have any further questions, I urge you to contact a member of the Commission staff. Sincerely, Daniel H. Lowenstein Chairman, for the Commission DHL:cjb The letter is from the California Federation of Republican Women, Southern Division, addressed to the Fair Political Practices Commission in Sacramento, California. The president of the organization, Mrs. Wayne W. Scott, writes about the organization's monthly bulletin mailed to officers, board of directors, and club presidents. According to Government Code, S 82041.5, mass mailings to 200 or more may apply to their organization's bulletin. The organization makes recommendations on pending legislation and encloses copies of their bulletins for this year. They request an opinion as to whether they are complying with the law and suggest sending only specific pages related to legislation and/or candidates rather than the entire bulletin. The regular mailing goes out under postal permit but is mailed as third class mail. Sincerely yours, Margaret Cutt Mrs. Wayne Scott President
